items.new_candidate: Dunnoway [unverified — sheriff name and county both uncertain per speaker]
items.evidence: Churchville himself flags both the county AND the sheriff identification as uncertain in this passage. In the transcript he says of the rape incident: 'We were in one community in southwest Georgia. And I don't even remember whether it was Lee, Lee, somewhere.' The notorious sheriffs documented in his actual area of SNCC work during 1962-63 were Z.T. Mathews (Terrell County), Fred Chappell (Sumter County/Americus, whom MLK called 'the meanest man in the world'), L. Warren 'Gator' Johnson (Baker County, who shot a handcuffed Charlie Ware four times), Cull Campbell (Dougherty County), and Laurie Pritchett (Albany city chief). For Lee County specifically, the SNCC Digital Gateway and One Person One Vote document that the Forrester family controlled the sheriff's office during this period — not anyone named Dunaway/Dunoway/Dunnoway. None of the documented Southwest Georgia sheriffs phonetically map to 'Dunnoway,' and no external source I searched (SNCC Digital Gateway, CRMVet oral histories, Georgia Encyclopedia, Civil Rights Cold Cases) places anyone by that surname in law enforcement in this region during this era. Given (a) Churchville's own disclaimed memory of the county, (b) no external corroboration of the name, and (c) the documented Lee County sheriff was a Forrester, retaining the literal Whisper rendering with an [unverified] flag is the Smithsonian-grade safe choice rather than inventing a canonical name or substituting one of the known sheriffs without textual basis.
